The Amazon Barclaycard

4.3 out of 5 stars5,807

Get up to £30 in Amazon Gift Cards¹
Representative 28.9% APR variable.

You'll get a £20 Amazon Gift Card if approved, plus activate your Amazon Barclaycard in the Barclays or Barclaycard app* for an extra £10 Amazon Gift Card.
Subject to financial status. Amazon EU S.A.R.L. is a credit broker not a lender, Barclays is the lender.

Earn 1%

back in rewards when you shop on Amazon.²

Earn 0.5%

back when you shop everywhere else.²

Log in to apply

Unfortunately, Apple Pay is unavailable for the Amazon Barclaycard.

Get 1% back in rewards when you shop on Amazon.²
Amazon Barclaycard Reward Rules

Get 0.5% back when you shop everywhere else.²
Amazon Barclaycard Reward Rules

Prime Benefits

Prime members get 2% back in rewards during eligible Amazon key shopping events, like Prime Day.³

Purchase Offer

Get 0% interest on all purchases for 6 months from the date you open your account.

No Card Fees

No monthly or annual fees.

The approval of your application for the Amazon Barclaycard credit card depends on your financial circumstances and borrowing history, so do the terms you may be offered. The interest rates may differ from those shown. Terms & Conditions. See a summary of key product features in the Summary of rates.

Rewards on your
everyday spending

Earn Amazon Rewards every time you pay with your Amazon Barclaycard and watch them grow in the Barclays or Barclaycard app.* When you’ve earned £5 in rewards, you can transfer a £5 Amazon Gift Card to your Amazon account – or let them keep growing. Amazon Barclaycard Reward Rules

Alexa, what are
my Prime benefits?

Get a boost on rewards as a thank you for being a Prime member during eligible Amazon key shopping events. Eligible key shopping events will be selected and displayed specifically for the Amazon Barclaycard at our discretion.

• 2% back in rewards when shopping on Amazon during eligible Amazon key shopping events like Prime Day.³ Amazon Barclaycard Reward Rules

Activate your card
in the app*

Download the Barclays or Barclaycard app to manage and use your account. It's safe, secure and free to download from the App Store or Google Play.

  • Activate your card in the app and get a £10 Amazon Gift Card
  • View your digital card details and use it to spend online
  • Check your rewards balance
  • View your statement
  • Set up or change your payment details and monthly payment date

*You need to be 18 or over to access this product or service using the app. Terms and conditions apply.

Features and benefits of
your Amazon Barclaycard


This is an app-first account

You’ll need the Barclays or Barclaycard app* to activate, use and manage your account. You can also manage some parts of your account in Barclays Online Banking on your laptop or desktop PC.⁴

Fraud Protection 24/7

Whether you’re buying on the high street, online or overseas, Barclaycard's state of the art detection system monitors around the clock to stop fraud in its tracks.

Protection on purchases

You could be covered if there’s a problem with some Barclaycard purchases – a feature of all UK credit cards. Please note: purchase protection may not be available where third party sellers sell goods on Amazon.co.uk. Find out more about purchase protection.

Entertainment perks.
Representative 28.9% APR variable

From specially reserved tickets at iconic venues across the UK, to tickets for less at a range of selected events, enjoy the best in live experiences with Barclaycard. Barclaycard Entertainment T&Cs.

Digital Wallet ready

Add to your Google Wallet, to make contactless payments and check out quickly and securely on your favourite apps and sites.
Unfortunately, Apple Pay is unavailable for the Amazon Barclaycard.

How to apply


1. Check

Check your eligibility to find out if you are likely to be approved without affecting your credit rating.

2. Apply

If approved by Barclaycard, your digital card is ready to be used on Amazon.co.uk and from the Barclays or Barclaycard app* right away. Your plastic card and PIN will arrive separately in the post within 9 working days.

3. Activate

After successful application and card activation, we'll add your new card automatically to your Amazon account as your default payment option. Download the Barclays or Barclaycard app* to start spending, and to manage your Barclaycard account. With your new card, you can start earning rewards while shopping on Amazon.co.uk, online and anywhere Visa is accepted.

Learn more about how credit cards work

Customers like you are asking

What important information about The Amazon Barclaycard do I need to know?

  • Your standard and cash interest rates will move up and down in line with the Bank of England Base Rate or for other reasons that Barclaycard have explained in your Terms & Conditions. Base Rate changes will not affect your promotional interest rates. To learn more visit barclaycard.co.uk/understanding-interest
  • Unfortunately, Apple Pay is unavailable for the Amazon Barclaycard. You can still use your card via contactless, or on Google Pay if you have an Android device
  • This is an app-first account. You’ll need to use the Barclays or Barclaycard app to activate, use and manage your account*
  • Your account will be set up with digital statements. These can be found in your Barclays or Barclaycard app*
  • It is not possible to add an additional cardholder right now.
  • The Amazon Barclaycard introductory 0% purchase offer is only available to new customers and Barclaycard reserve the right to withdraw the offer at any time

Is The Amazon Barclaycard suitable for me?

To be approved for the Amazon Barclaycard you need to:

  • Be 18 years old or over and a UK resident
  • Have been living permanently at a UK address for at least 2 years
  • Be able to afford repayments on any outstanding credit commitments
  • Have not closed a Barclaycard in the past 6 months
  • Have no Individual Voluntary Agreements, County Court Judgements or declarations of bankruptcy within the past 6 years


You’re more likely to be approved if you:

  • Always make payments on time
  • Have at least 4 years’ experience of managing credit
  • Have a personal income of more than £20,000 a year
  • Pass a ‘soft’ (eligibility) credit check. This helps us estimate the credit limit you could get and won’t affect your credit score

You'll need to activate, use and manage your account in the Barclays or Barclaycard app.* To get the app, you'll need a compatible device and internet access. Go to your usual app store to check if your device is compatible.


Once you're set up you can also manage some parts of your account, including your statements and Amazon Rewards, in Barclays Online Banking on your laptop or desktop PC.⁴ Barclaycard will send you an email with your Barclays Online Banking membership number and more information once your application has been approved.


The Amazon Barclaycard is a Visa credit card. This means you can use it for your everyday spending, as well as your Amazon spending, and then you settle your bill later. The rewards you earn on this product can only be redeemed as Amazon Gift Cards which are applied to your Amazon.co.uk account on request. If this doesn't sound right for you, there may be other lending options available which better suit your needs including Instalments by Barclays.

What will I need to hand when I apply?

  • Address details for the last 2 years
  • Phone number and email address
  • Current employment details
  • Details of your annual income before tax
  • Details of your monthly income after tax
  • A good idea about your monthly outgoings
  • Your sort code and account number (if you have a Barclays current account)


An understanding of how fees and offers work can help you manage your Barclaycard account, so make sure you’re up to speed on the types of fees Barclaycard may charge and how promotional rates work.

How do I apply for The Amazon Barclaycard?

Click the ‘Check eligibility & apply’ button above to start an application for the Amazon Barclaycard. Applications typically only take a few minutes to complete, and a decision may be made by Barclaycard immediately, or in some cases may take up to 5-7 working days.

Can I switch to The Amazon Barclaycard if I already have a Barclaycard?

Yes, you can apply to switch to the Amazon Barclaycard. You'll be able to compare the Amazon Barclaycard's features with your current card before deciding whether to switch. You should consider the following points before deciding.

  • You'll lose any unused promotional offers on your current card
  • You can redeem any rewards on your current card before switching. If you don't, Barclaycard will redeem them automatically on the anniversary of you opening that card. If you have a Barclaycard Avios, they will transfer any Avios you've collected as usual
  • If Barclaycard approves your switch, they will close your current Barclaycard straight away and transfer your balance to the new card
  • The interest rate (APR) you pay on your total balance after switching might be different than your current card
  • You won't be able to switch back to your current card, have more than one Barclaycard or have any additional cardholders
  • Barclaycard doesn't check your credit score but they will have to tell credit reference agencies when you close a card and open a new one. This will affect your credit score.
  • You can't switch to the Amazon Barclaycard if your current card balance is more than your credit limit, or if you already have more than one Barclaycard.

    • How do I register for the Barclaycard app and activate my Amazon Barclaycard account?

    If you're approved for an Amazon Barclaycard, you'll need to activate your account in the Barclays or Barclaycard app* before you can start spending.

    First, you'll need to download the app from your usual app store if you don't already have it. Once it's downloaded, open the app, select 'Register with my Amazon Barclaycard' and follow the instructions.

    To register for the app, you'll need to use the same UK mobile phone number you gave us in your application, and either:

  • Your passport or a UK or EU driving licence (but not from Romania) and your phone or tablet with camera access to verify your identity
    or
  • Your card and PIN.

    Once that's done, or if you already have the app, you can select your Amazon Barclaycard on the app home screen to activate your account.

    Once it's activated, your Amazon Barclaycard will automatically be added to your Amazon account as your default payment method. You'll also be able to add it to Google Pay using the card details shown in the app and use your card online straight away.

    In some cases, it might take up to 24 hours for your Amazon Barclaycard account to appear in the app. If you can't see your account after that time, contact Barclaycard Customer Services on 0800 151 0157. Open from 8am to 8pm Monday to Friday, and 8am to 6pm on Saturday and Sunday. Call charges may apply.

    • When will I receive my welcome gift card and activation gift card?

    You’ll receive an email from Barclaycard if they approve your application for the Amazon Barclaycard. You’ll receive your welcome £20 Amazon Gift Card upon approval, and an additional £10 Amazon Gift Card when you activate your Amazon Barclaycard in the Barclays or Barclaycard app.¹ Each Amazon Gift Card will be added to the same Amazon.co.uk account used in your Amazon Barclaycard account application. To manage your Amazon Gift Card, visit Your Amazon Gift Card Balance page.

    How do I earn rewards with The Amazon Barclaycard?

    Get 1% back in rewards when you shop on Amazon, and 0.5% back everywhere else (0.5% changes to 0.25% after 12 months from the date you open your card account). Amazon Barclaycard Reward Rules. If you’re a Prime member, you’ll get 2% back when you shop on Amazon during eligible key shopping events, like Prime Day. Eligible key shopping events will be selected and displayed specifically for the Amazon Barclaycard at our discretion. The increased rewards rate will not apply to all Amazon shopping events.

    You’ll earn the 'shop on Amazon' rewards rate for purchases made at the following Amazon-supported channels and entities: Amazon Retail, Amazon Marketplace, Amazon Fresh, Amazon Music, Whole Foods, Audible, Kindle, Twitch, Amazon Prime Video, Amazon Luna, Amazon Blink, AWS Training UK, Amazon Bulk Liquidations, and Amazon Trade In UK.

    You’ll collect Amazon Rewards on new purchases, but not on balance transfers, money transfers and transactions that make up your cash balance. These include cash withdrawals, wire transfers and buying currency. You won’t collect rewards for any transactions that are later returned or refunded; or for any transactions made via a third party card or account such as Curve or PayPal. You also need to keep to the terms and conditions of your account and stay within your credit limit. You can find out more about collecting and spending Amazon Rewards, including which purchases are eligible by viewing the Amazon Barclaycard Reward Rules.

    How do I access my rewards?

    Your Amazon Rewards can be viewed in the Barclays or Barclaycard app.*

    When you’ve earned £5 in Amazon Rewards, you can transfer an Amazon Gift Card to your linked Amazon.co.uk account – or let them keep growing. As you carry on earning, you can transfer gift cards in multiples of £5 using the Barclays or Barclaycard app. So, if you have a rewards balance of £17.50, you could transfer £15 as gift cards, leaving £2.50 in your rewards balance. You’ll find your gift cards in your linked Amazon.co.uk account within 2 hours of transferring them. You can apply them to your purchases when you check out at Amazon – it’s that simple.

    Your Amazon Gift Cards will be redeemed to your linked Amazon.co.uk account. If you get a new Amazon account or would like to change which Amazon account your rewards are sent to, you can do this by adding the Amazon Barclaycard to your Amazon Wallet and follow the instructions to “Claim a card” via your My Amazon Barclaycard page.

    Who do I contact with credit limit questions?

    To discuss any questions relating to your Amazon Barclaycard credit limit, contact Barclaycard Customer Services on 0800 151 0157. Open from 8am to 8pm Monday to Friday, and 8am to 6pm on Saturday and Sunday. Call charges may apply. Alternatively, send a message from the ‘Help’ section of the Barclays or Barclaycard app.

    Why was my application declined by Barclaycard?

    If you would like to talk to Barclaycard about your declined application, contact Barclaycard Customer Services on 0800 151 0157. Open from 8am to 8pm Monday to Friday, and 8am to 6pm on Saturday and Sunday. Call charges may apply.

    Who do I contact for further help?

    Barclaycard can help if you need this information in a different way or would like some extra support with your banking. If you have a disability, a physical or mental health condition, or anything else that means you need extra support when interacting with us, let Barclaycard know.

    Contact Barclaycard Customer Services on 0800 151 0157. Open from 8am to 8pm Monday to Friday, and 8am to 6pm on Saturday and Sunday. Call charges may apply.

    How are customer reviews used?

    Amazon and Barclays use customer reviews about the Amazon Barclaycard to improve customer experience and address complaints. Cardholders posting reviews may be identified to Barclays for this purpose. For more information on how information about you is used and shared by Amazon, please see the Amazon Privacy Notice. For more information about customer reviews, please see Understanding Customer Reviews and Ratings.

    How are verified reviews used?

    Amazon uses verified customer reviews to help customers learn more about the product and decide whether it is the right product for them. We check if the reviewer is a cardmember. If we confirm the customer is a cardmember, we label the review with Verified Cardmember. Reviews without this label can also be helpful and will be displayed. To calculate the overall star rating and percentage breakdown by star, we don't use a simple average. Instead, our system considers factors like how recent a review is whether the item was bought on Amazon, and verified cardmember status to help customers make informed decisions. It also analyses reviews to verify trustworthiness. For more information about customer reviews, please see Understanding Verified Reviews and Ratings.

    ¹ A £20 Amazon Gift Card will be added to your linked Amazon.co.uk account upon approval for The Amazon Barclaycard, and an additional £10 Amazon Gift Card will be added to your linked Amazon.co.uk account after activating your card on the Barclays or Barclaycard app. To find out how to activate your card once approved, please see the 'How do I register for the Barclaycard app and activate my Amazon Barclaycard?' FAQ above. This offer is available for new customers only.

    ² Get 1% back in rewards when you shop on Amazon and 0.5% back everywhere else. 0.5% changes to 0.25% after 12 months from the date you open your card account. Amazon Barclaycard Reward Rules

    ³ Amazon Prime members get 2% back in rewards when you shop on Amazon during eligible key shopping events, like Prime Day. Eligible key shopping events will be selected and displayed specifically for the Amazon Barclaycard at our discretion. The increased rewards rate will not apply to all Amazon shopping events.

    You’ll earn the 'shop on Amazon' rewards rate for purchases made at the following Amazon-supported channels and entities: Amazon Retail, Amazon Marketplace, Amazon Fresh, Amazon Music, Whole Foods, Audible, Kindle, Twitch, Amazon Prime Video, Amazon Luna, Amazon Blink, AWS Training UK, Amazon Bulk Liquidations, and Amazon Trade In UK.

    ⁴ In Barclays Online Banking you can view statements and transactions, make a payment by debit card, view and redeem Amazon Rewards, and report your card damaged, lost or stolen. You can do all of this and more in the app.

    The retailer, Amazon EU S.A.R.L., acts as a credit broker and not the lender, and is authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (Financial Services Register number: 746485). Registered in England with registered number: BR017427. UK office address: 1 Principal Place, London EC2A 2FA. Barclaycard is a trading name of Barclays Bank UK PLC. Barclays Bank UK PLC acts as the lender for The Amazon Barclaycard, and is authorised by the Prudential Regulation Authority and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority and the Prudential Regulation Authority (Financial Services Register number: 759676). Registered in England with registered number: 9740322. Registered office: 1 Churchill Place, London, E14 5HP.

    How customer reviews and ratings work

    Customer Reviews, including Product Star Ratings, help customers to learn more about the product and decide whether it is the right product for them.

    To calculate the overall star rating and percentage breakdown by star, we don’t use a simple average. Instead, our system considers things like how recent a review is and if the reviewer bought the item on Amazon. It also analyses reviews to verify trustworthiness.

    Learn more how customers reviews work on Amazon

    Customers say

    Customers find the Amazon Barclaycard easy to use and appreciate its functionality, particularly how it works seamlessly with Amazon Prime accounts. The card offers excellent rewards with money to spend on Amazon, and customers like its app features, with one noting it keeps them fully updated. While customers find it very useful for Amazon purchases and consider it an excellent value for money, they report issues with Apple Pay compatibility, as the card doesn't support this feature.

    • James Whymark
      5.0 out of 5 stars Great credit card from Barclays and Amazon
      Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 10 May 2026
      Verified Cardmember
      Easy to set up the card to my Amazon account and quick and straightforward to pay each month. The rewards add up really quick too. Really pleased.

       Report
    • Anish
      5.0 out of 5 stars Simple rewards card for Amazon users
      Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 27 April 2026
      Verified Cardmember
      I’ve been using the Amazon Barclaycard alongside my existing HSBC credit card, and so far it has been a good addition. The main benefit is earning rewards on Amazon purchases, which works well if you shop there regularly.

      The application process was straightforward, and the app is easy to use for tracking spending and payments. I only use it for small purchases (£100–£150 occasionally) and pay the full balance each month, so it’s been easy to manage.

      It’s not the most powerful rewards card overall, but for regular Amazon users, the cashback adds up over time.

      Just make sure to pay on time and stay within your limit, as with any credit card.

      Overall, a simple and useful card if you already shop on Amazon.
      Read more
      6 people found this helpful
      Helpful
       Report
    • Mr Rizwan Tariq
      5.0 out of 5 stars Amazon Barclaycard: Effortless Spending, Real Rewards, Exceptional Experience
      Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 18 March 2026
      Verified Cardmember
      My experience with the Amazon Barclaycard has been nothing short of exceptional. From the moment I started using it, the entire process from application to everyday use has been seamless, intuitive, and genuinely rewarding.

      What truly stands out is the effortless integration with Amazon purchases. Every transaction feels purposeful, with cashback and rewards accumulating in a way that actually feels meaningful rather than gimmicky. It transforms routine spending into something far more satisfying, especially for anyone who regularly shops on Amazon.

      The user experience is equally impressive. The app is clean, responsive, and easy to navigate, giving full control over spending, payments, and tracking rewards in real time. Everything is transparent, which builds a strong sense of trust and reliability something that’s crucial when it comes to financial products.

      Customer service has also proven to be highly efficient and professional. Any queries are handled promptly, with clear and helpful responses that make you feel valued as a customer rather than just another account number.

      Overall, the Amazon Barclaycard delivers exactly what it promises and then some. It combines convenience, tangible rewards, and a smooth digital experience into one powerful package. For anyone looking to enhance their everyday spending while earning genuine benefits, this card is an outstanding choice.
      Read more

    • Mr T Kerry
      4.0 out of 5 stars Pretty standard credit card, prime event points soon stack up
      Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 20 January 2026
      Verified Cardmember
      Mostly been a good experience, got the card number a week or so before getting the card (royal mail not great in our area) so was able to start spending haha. The only two downsides so far - I've not been able to set up an additional card holder (think it's being worked on). The other small teething issue was working out when the promo bonus would apply for and had to dig through a faqs to find when the Amazon bonus would be applied for promo events (e.g. black Friday) id done most of my shopping during the event, but before the Barclaycard promo started - so make sure you read up before spending during these and don't just assume like I did. On the positive side, I've redeemed one gift card already, that process was easy and worked seemlessly from the Barclays app, applying the gift card in my Amazon account.
      Read more
      28 people found this helpful
      Helpful

    • Ritchie
      5.0 out of 5 stars Trust and ease of use
      Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 14 May 2026
      Verified Cardmember
      You can trust amazon barclaycard

      I feel very safe using it with no hassle and the money gained to spend at amazon is a bonus

    • Jamie G
      3.0 out of 5 stars Card is fine, once everything arrives that's needed to set it up.
      Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 23 April 2026
      Verified Cardmember
      The card is fine, once everything turns up. The onboarding experience leaves a lot to be desired. For example, when you receive the card it comes with a letter asking you to install the app and create an account. Only you can't, because you have to wait for your PIN code to be mailed to you separately. Neither the letter or app make this clear. It just feels like a bit disconnected, like somebody forgot to test the entire user journey. The app itself is a bit weird too. For example: if I enter a screen to view settings and then click the Android back button (instead of the back button in the app) it asks me if I want to cancel changes when I haven't made any.
      Read more
